item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The line about handing over your biomass read like a really close paraphrase from this scene from the Animatrix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Other articles have tackled the ideas of endless nothingness and horrific afterlives better than this, and without taking as long to get to the interesting parts.

item.title: Good SCP
item.content:encoded: Nice work I like it. Your writing is concise yet entertaining and the overall narrative ties in well with 2718. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a lot of 2718, which does the 'death is incredibly horrible for everyone and you should do anything to avoid it' bit, and aside from that all it has going for it is a crystal that makes zombies.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I ultimately came down to a no-vote (neutral vote) on this.

I think the main problem here is that the core concept leans heavily on a few site cliches, and doesn't do enough to build beyond that. As a fan of technical scips, the tight tone and some interesting nuances to the effect helped sell me a bit on the idea (the experiment logs in particular have some good moments of black humor and creepiness), but ultimately there's no moment where I find myself saying "oh?…ooohh…" or staring at the screen processing the full ramifications of what I just read.

I'm not saying this can't work, but it might be one to put on the back-burner while you work on something more off the beaten trail; some of the best twists come from letting an idea sit for a while until you get one of those "Oh duh, that's what I should do!" moments.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hi. Thank you for your thoughts, I've taken them all on board.

Very strange that only O5 council members can access the SCP's containment
seeing how they generally aren't supposed to come in contact with anomalies.

In my mind, the O5 council members wouldn't be coming into contact with the object, only very select staff. I wrote it in accordance with what is stated in the Guide Hub:

Level 5 security clearances are given to the highest-ranking administrative
personnel within the Foundation and grant effectively unlimited access to all
strategic and otherwise sensitive data. Level 5 security clearances are typically
only granted to O5 Council members and selected staff.

This sentence doesn't make sense to me. Is it a crystal with a
cognitohazardous effect, because this sentence seems to imply that the
crystal itself is like the physical embodiment of the cognitohazard or something?

Sorry if it wasn't clear. What I meant it to mean was that the crystal is a cognitohazard if you touch it, thereby a "tactile cognitohazard" (although it was eventually discovered physical touch isn't necessary, which is why it was struck through.)

The description is simply put, painful to get through. I'd say you could cut
at least half of those words out and it'd still make just as much sense. There
is no hook; some kind of mystery in the conprocs to pull you through all of that.

Noted; I'll try to be more succinct in future. The hook was supposed to be "I wonder what these people are seeing after they die? Why won't they talk about it?" Unfortunately, it doesn't look like it was that interesting, or I just didn't write it in an interesting way, or the concept itself is just inherently boring.

Anyway, thanks for all your feedback. Hopefully I'll do better next time!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Here are my thoughts on 5944;

Line by Line
Line by Line

openable only to on-site staff with Level 5 clearance or above

Very strange that only O5 council members can access the SCP's containment seeing how they generally aren't supposed to come in contact with anomalies.

SCP-5944 is a tactile cognitohazard in the form of an anomalous translucent crystal.

This sentence doesn't make sense to me. Is it a crystal with a cognitohazardous effect, because this sentence seems to imply that the crystal itself is like the physical embodiment of the cognitohazard or something?

General Thoughts
General Thoughts

The description is simply put, painful to get through. I'd say you could cut at least half of those words out and it'd still make just as much sense. There is no hook; some kind of mystery in the conprocs to pull you through all of that. By the second paragraph, I already understood what it was. None of the rest was necessary.

The anomaly itself is generic and boring. It's a crystal that turns people into zombies, and this skip does nothing interesting with it. The interview and experiment logs all blur together. It's just zombies being zombies. It's exactly what I expected based on the description. There is no twist or subversion. It doesn't lead anywhere. I suppose the final addendum is supposed to be some kind of conclusion but I honestly have no idea what it means.

I can't really suggest specific changes because I think the idea itself needs major reworking. I recommend checking out the Ideas Forum if you want some advice from experienced authors.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I can see this article is at about a 50/50 split right know between likes and dislikes. Judging the current trajectory, it'll probably slowly slip into the negatives. I know nobody is expected nor required to justify their votes, but I'd appreciate it if you could find the time to voice what didn't work for you, or what I could've done better.

This is my first SCP (and my first time writing fiction). I have a background in scientific writing, so I I didn't have too much trouble with the clinical tone. Is the narrative not strong enough? I really would like to improve and contribute something great to the site.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded: Thank you, I'm sorry for the confusion.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded:
Snappygoat mentions that they got the image from Pixabay. However, this was uploaded to Pixabay prior to the license change, so it is still licensed under CC 0 Public Domain.

Image has been restored.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded:
The original image I found was here on Snappygoat: https://snappygoat.com/s/?q=blue+crystal#2dee414b9091f75d5847989aed77f581f7b889b2,1,4658.

However, I linked to the pixabay one instead. I apologise if it wasn't CC complaint.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded: The image is hosted on Pixabay and is under the Pixabay License, which is not compliant with CC BY SA 3.0.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded: This image is hosted on Snappygoat, I was under the impression these were CC compliant?

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I did a tiny little fix. You wrote:

A full recovery from SCP-5944’s cognitohazardous effects are possible, but highly variable.

But it should be "is possible" not "are possible" because the thing that is possible is "a full recovery" (singular) not "cognitohazardous effects" (plural)

So I've already gone in and fixed it, but since you said it's your first time I wanted to also pop in and explain the reason for it in detail so that you'd know why someone changed your words.

item.title: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded: I have removed the image from the article due to it not being compliant with the wiki's own license. For more information, you can read this guide and familiarize yourself with what you can and cannot use on the wiki.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
First time author; figured I might as well see if this sinks or swims before moving on.

Image: https://pixabay.com/photos/crystal-mineral-glass-ball-glass-162608/
